title: The Pathless Path
author: Paul Millerd
subline: Imagining a new story for work and life.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A field of dashed/dotted path lines that all converge to a center — but the center is BLANK. The path doesn't lead to a destination. One red dot is the only solid mark. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1750)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="6 8">
    <path d="M-700 -700 L -100 -100"/>
    <path d="M700 -700 L 100 -100"/>
    <path d="M-700 0 L -100 0"/>
    <path d="M700 0 L 100 0"/>
    <path d="M-700 700 L -100 100"/>
    <path d="M700 700 L 100 100"/>
    <path d="M0 -700 L 0 -100"/>
    <path d="M0 700 L 0 100"/>
    <path d="M-700 -350 L -100 -50"/>
    <path d="M700 -350 L 100 -50"/>
    <path d="M-700 350 L -100 50"/>
    <path d="M700 350 L 100 50"/>
  </g>
  <g transform="translate(800 1750)">
    <!-- Single red dot at the convergence — the only solid mark in a sea of dashed paths -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="40" y="6" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#fc1c46">YOU</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: All paths converge to a single point — but the point is blank. Dashed lines from every direction; a single red dot at the center labeled 'YOU.' The book's whole thesis: the convergence is you, not a destination. 480px whisper title dominates the top. Single red = the walker.
